title: Ambulia conferta
taxonomy: Ambulia conferta belongs to the family Primulaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the primrose or primula family. This family is part of the order Ericales, a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The genus Ambulia is a group of plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions, and are often found in wetland environments. Ambulia conferta is a type of flowering plant that is native to Southeast Asia, where it can be found growing in the wild. The classification of Ambulia conferta reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history, and provides a framework for understanding its relationships to other plants in the Primulaceae family.
